beats.narration: Postcleaning washes the indication down the drain forever. The only thing that survives the examination is the record — so the report is the product.

payload.title: THE RECORD IS THE PRODUCT
payload.sub: The indication is gone. The report is what an auditor reads.

beats.narration: A complete report answers five questions: what was examined, how it was processed, under what conditions, what was found, and who signs for it.

payload.heading: Five questions every report answers
payload.note: WHAT · HOW · CONDITIONS · WHAT WAS FOUND · WHO SAYS SO

beats.narration: No entry stands alone. Every value points to evidence someone else can pull — materials to batch certs, readings to calibration, signatures to certs.
So write it straight. Record what you measured, not what the spec wanted. Locate from a datum. Correct with one line, never correction fluid.

payload.heading: How a Level II writes an entry

chips.s: The meter read it; write that number

beats.narration: Write every report expecting an auditor to walk each entry back to objective evidence — because one day, one will.

payload.sub: If it is not written down, it did not happen.
